title: Russia's Seaborne Oil Flows Changing Patterns
section_path: Russia's Seaborne Oil Flows Changing Patterns
section_path_text: Russia's Seaborne Oil Flows Changing Patterns
summary:
Main
Russia's seaborne crude oil flows are trying to find new destination countries, while the US has already set an embargo on Russian oil. is in the process of discussions for final approval by all member countries in the next six months with wavering uncertainty for potential alternative sources to replace the Russian crude oil. In March , the situation was too premature for the potential implications of sanctions on the global oil production and seaborne transportation; and whether there will be decisions from Western countries to move forward with drastic actions on diminishing Russian oil supplies.

children.title: Main
children.summary: Russia's seaborne crude oil flows are trying to find new destination countries, while the US has already set an embargo on Russian oil. is in the process of discussions for final approval by all member countries in the next six months with wavering uncertainty for potential alternative sources to replace the Russian crude oil.

children.title: Oil Flows - The Worldwide Picture 2020 -2022
children.summary: We see a minor decrease of less than 1% in oil flows from March to April. Overall, the first quarter of the year ended with no significant decrease in the daily average volume of flows amid the increased geopolitical uncertainty.

children.title: Oil flows to the U.S. Destination Country | Their dependence on Russian supply
section_path: Oil flows to the U.S. Destination Country | Their dependence on Russian supply
children.section_path_text: Oil flows to the U.S. Destination Country | Their dependence on Russian supply
children.summary: After the official announcement of the U.S. ban on Russian oil imports, the daily exported volume of crude flows from Russia to the U.S.

children.title: Oil Flows to Europe
children.summary: Overall oil flows to European destinations are steadily increasing following the ending of the first six months of last year (Image 6). The daily average volume of flows to Europe reached April one of the highest points over the previous twelve months, while the Russian oil embargo is not yet in the final approval stage by all member states.

children.title: Changing patterns in the top European origin and destination countries
section_path: Changing patterns in the top European origin and destination countries
children.section_path_text: Changing patterns in the top European origin and destination countries
children.summary: The latest announcement of Europe for the full force Russian oil embargo brought a new wave of flows from Norway to Europe, where we can view (Image 9) an increasing trend of daily cargo volume within February-March-April. It seems that Norway will play a substantial role in the next few months in the replacement of Russian oil flows to Europe.

children.title: What can be the future of the oil market? Can Europe and other nations replace Russian crude oil?
section_path: What can be the future of the oil market? Can Europe and other nations replace Russian crude oil?
children.section_path_text: What can be the future of the oil market? Can Europe and other nations replace Russian crude oil?
children.summary: It is a fact that the transition is already in the pipeline, but the scenarios for a full replacement are still in doubt as Russia is one of the most important major players in the energy market. could simultaneously increase crude oil sales from their national strategic stocks to lessen the impact of any further restrictions on Russian crude oil imports to the G-7.

Japan agrees with Britain, Canada, France on G-7 unity against Russia
WEISSENHAUS, Germany - Japanese Foreign Minister Yoshimasa Hayashi on Thursday agreed with his counterparts from Britain, Canada and France on the importance of the Group of Seven countries standing in unity to address Russia's invasion of Ukraine. This article is no longer available.
